signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Carry + Geopolitics
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.11 — 160.00 remains the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on this sustained break. 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid, but headlines of an interim US-Iran peace deal introduce downside volatility. JPY COT LF net short -115,036 (crowded carry) faces squeeze risk if geopolitical de-escalation or BoJ hike bias (16 Jun) triggers a cover. Carry dominates until VIX > 25.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Retail Divergence + Oil + Technicals
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3984 testing the 1.4000 psychological resistance. Retail sentiment is at an extreme 92% short, diverging from the COT LF net short of -60,671 (specs also short). This institutional-retail alignment in fading the move suggests a squeeze higher if 1.4000 breaks. Lower WTI oil at $84.9 and a +150bp USD carry advantage provide fundamental support for the pair despite overbought RSI signals.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Premium + CESI Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD IV at 32.1% vs HV30 at 8.2% generates a massive +23.9% Vol Risk Premium, signaling extreme hedging demand for downside protection. This aligns with a negative CESI (net -9 misses) and a 25d RR of -1.05 (put skew). Despite a +60bp AUD carry advantage, the options market is pricing significant tail risk. Watch for the RBA meeting on 16 Jun to either validate the carry bid or trigger a vol-led liquidation.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Volatility Regime
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1579 remains under pressure from a +150bp USD carry advantage. COT LF net short -49,290 (37.4% long) shows specs are positioned for further downside, supported by a positive USD CESI (+30 beats). However, a +2.9% VRP (IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.2%) indicates the market is paying a premium for protection, suggesting late-trend caution. Watch the Fed meeting on 17 Jun for a shift in the real rate differential (+1.31% USD vs +0.03% EUR).

signals.title: GBP/USD — Carry Neutrality + Data Resilience
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3410 is fundamentally supported by a neutral 0bp carry spread (Fed 3.75% vs BoE 3.75%) and a positive real rate of +0.77%. COT LF net long +21,961 (61.7% long) reflects speculative confidence in UK growth resilience. Options market remains cautious with a -0.71 25d RR put skew, but a high VRP of +5.0% suggests this is hedging rather than a directional reversal. Watch the BoE meeting on 18 Jun for hold confirmation.
